steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage has just defined the soft/hard distinction by origin (grown/farmed vs. mined/drilled), not by physical durability. The blank introduces a sentence that applies this definition to pine lumber — which is grown, so it counts as soft even though it is physically sturdy. The transition must signal that what follows flows directly from the definition just stated.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Though it is grown from the earth,': this concedes the grown-origin fact as if it were a counterpoint, implying pine lumber should not be soft — the opposite of what the passage says. ✗
  • Choice B — 'In a notable exception to this rule,': pine lumber is not an exception; it is a straightforward application of the rule. ✗
  • Choice C — 'By this definition,': directly links the sentence back to the origin-based definition just given and shows that pine lumber fits the rule logically. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Summarizing this principle,': the sentence is not a summary of the preceding idea — it is an illustration of how the rule applies to a specific case. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
